summary: Reviewers consistently praise the Aero Lite's low weight, calling it one of the lightest screws in its class and a natural pick for fast, high-volume placements on alpine routes where screw count matters more than raw durability. Durability draws more mixed comments: the aluminum tube holds up fine in soft to moderate ice but several reviewers caution against repeated placements in hard or rock-scraped ice, where the teeth are noted to dull faster than steel screws. Reviewers rate the starting bite and cranking speed as adequate but not as confidence-inspiring as Petzl's Speed thread pattern on hard water ice.

review_links.key_takeaway: Excellent weight-to-strength ratio for alpine racks; teeth dull faster than steel on hard ice.
Aluminum tube holds up well across seasons of moderate ice but isn't the first choice for sustained hard-ice pitches.
